**3. Tables:**

**Table 1: Sample blends of Sasol synthetic kerosene**

| Synthetic kerosene stream | FSJF Blend 1 | FSJF Blend 2 | FSJF Blend 3 | FSJF Blend 4 | Natref Jet A-1 |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| Heavy naphtha 1 | 15 | 15 | 13 | 59 | - |
| Light distillate 1 | 18 | - | 10 | - |  -|
| Light distillate 2 | - | 30 | 20 | 36 |  -|
| Isoparaffinic kerosene | 67 | 55 | 57 | 5 |  -|

| Chemistry | FSJF Blend 1 | FSJF Blend 2 | FSJF Blend 3 | FSJF Blend 4 | Natref Jet A-1 |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| Aromatics, v % | 7.2 | 13.6 | 12.5 | 16.9 | 22.9 |
| Saturates, v % | 92.8 | 86.4 | 8705 | 83.1 | 77.1 |

**4. Diagrams:**

*   **Figure 1: Production schematic for Sasol fully synthetic jet fuel**
    *   Components and labels:
        *   H₂ + CO -> Coal Gasification -> Coal tar -> Naphtha / Distillate -> H'treat / H'crack -> Petrol / Diesel -> Heavy Naphtha #2 / Light Distillate #2
        *   H₂ + CO -> FISCHER-TROPSCH -> C₁ to C₄₀ HC Liquid
        * C3 & CA Olefins -> Polymer-ization -> Hydroge-nation -> Fraction-ation -> Petrol
        * Naphtha Cut -> Hydroge-nation -> Fraction-ation -> Petrol
        * Distillate cut -> Hydroge-nation -> Fraction-ation -> Diesel
        *   Iso-Paraffinic Kerosene
        *   Heavy Naphtha #1
        *   Light Distillate #1
        *   Blender
        *   Sasol Synthetic Jet Fuel

**5. Key numbers, constants, parameters**

*   Aromatics: 8% to 17%

title: 2010_properties_characteristics_and_performance_of_fully_synthetic_jets
text:
[VISUAL/EQUATION CONTENT - Page 4]
Here's the technical content extracted from the provided text and image, broken down by category:

**1. Mathematical Equations:**

NO_VISUAL_CONTENT

**2. Graphs/Charts:**

*   **Title:** JFTOT deposit map for conventional fuel with Code 3 deposit
*   **Axes:**
    *   X-axis: Distance Along Tube, mm
    *   Y-axis: Degrees Around Tube
    *   Z-axis: Depth of Deposit, nm
*   **Curves:** Shows the thickness of a "Typical Code 3 JFTOT Deposit" as a function of distance along the tube and degrees around the tube.
*   **Key Values:**  The graph shows a peak deposit thickness of about 120 nm (likely around 0 distance along the tube) and the deposit tapers off as the distance increases.

**3. Tables:**

NO_VISUAL_CONTENT

**4. Diagrams:**

NO_VISUAL_CONTENT

**5. Key Numbers, Constants, Parameters:**

*   Aromatics limited to 25 vol% in jet fuel
*   Minimum aromatic level of 8 vol% in semisynthetic jet fuel (DEF STAN 91-91)
*   Test fuel soak temperature: 23.9 ± 2.8°C (75 ± 5°F)
*   14-day soak duration
*   O-ring volume increase tolerance for static applications: 50%
*   O-ring volume increase tolerance for dynamic applications: 15-20%
*   Code 3 deposits have a max thickness of approximately 120 nm
*   Code 3 deposit by conventional fuels has a thickness of approximatelty 20nm
*   Code 3 deposits start around 100 nm

title: 2010_properties_characteristics_and_performance_of_fully_synthetic_jets
text:
[VISUAL/EQUATION CONTENT - Page 10]
Here's the technical content extracted from the document:

**1. Mathematical Equations:**

NO_VISUAL_CONTENT

**2. Graphs/Charts:**

*   **Boiling Point Distribution of Test Fuel (Figure 13):**
    *   Axes:
        *   X-axis: PERCENTDISTILLED (no units specified, likely percentage).
        *   Y-axis: TEMPERATURE, °C
    *   Curves:
        *   Natref Jet A-1
        *   Synthetic test fuel
    *   Key:  Curves show the temperature vs. percentage distilled for two different fuels. Values are estimated from visual inspection, the Natref curve exhibits a higher distillation temperature at any given percentage.

**3. Tables:**

*   **Table 4: Selected Properties and Characteristics of the Test Fuel**

    | Fuel property           | Specification limits | Value |
    | ----------------------- | -------------------- | ----- |
    | Aromatics, vol %        | 8.0-25.0             | 10.9  |
    | Sulfur, wt %            | 0.3 max              | <0.01 |
    | Smoke point, mm         | 25.0 min             | 28    |
    | Viscosity, cS           | 8.0 max              | 4.09  |
    | Density, kg/m³          | 771.0-836.0          | 781.2 |
    | Freezing point, °C      | -47.0 max           | -55.0 |
    | Flash point, °C         | 38.0 min             | 51.0  |
    | Heat of combustion, kJ/kg | 42.8               | 43.7  |

**4. Diagrams:**

*   **Test Cycle for JT-9D Performance/Endurance Test of SSJF (Figure 14):**
    *   Components and Labels:
        *   Start
        *   Min. Idle Power (6 min)
        *   Cycle 1 thru 24 (Idle - 6 min, Cruise - 10 min, T/O - 2 min, Start over)
        *   90% Cruise Power
        *   Cruise Power (+4 Min, -4 Min)
        *   T/O Power
        *   Every 25th cycle (End with 10 min. minimum idle followed by a shutdown for 20 min. Start Cycles 1 to 25 over again)
        *   Min. Idle Power (6 min)
        *   Min. Idle Power (10 min)
        *   Shutdown

**5. Key Numbers, Constants, Parameters:**

*   Aromatics content 8.0-25.0 vol % (specification limits), actual value 10.9 vol %.
*   Sulfur content 0.3 wt % (max specification), actual value <0.01 wt %.
*   Smoke point 25 mm (min specification), actual value 28 mm.
*   Viscosity 8 cS (max specification), actual value 4.09 cS.
*   Density 771-836 kg/m³ (specification limits), actual value 781.2 kg/m³.
*   Freezing point -47 °C (max specification), actual value -55.0 °C.
*   Flash point 38 °C (min specification), actual value 51.0 °C.
*   Heat of combustion 42.

title: 2010_properties_characteristics_and_performance_of_fully_synthetic_jets

**1. Mathematical Equations:**

*   XPAR = (1 + Wf/Wa) / (ρaVa)

**2. Graphs/Charts:**

*   **Figure 15: Atomization of Sasol FSJF compared with Jet A at -40°C (-40°F) in a pressure atomizer**
    *   Axes:
        *   X-axis: ATOMIZER PRESSURE DROP, kPa
        *   Y-axis: RELATIVE SMD, SMD/SMD 7024-Type II
    *   Curves:
        *   Viscor
        *   Jet A
        *   Sasol FSJF
    *   Key Values:
        *   Approximately read values where pressure drop is 400, 800, and 1200kPa: Viscor SMD decreasing from 1.75 to 1.3 to 1.1; Jet A SMD decreasing from 1.9 to 1.4 to 1.2; Sasol FSJF decreasing from 1.6 to 1.4, the 1.3

**3. Tables:**

*   **Table 5: Physical properties of test fluids for atomization**

    | Test fuel | Specific gravity At 25°C | Specific gravity At test temp.| Viscosity At 25°C | Viscosity Test temp. | Surface tension At 25°C | Surface tension At test temp.|
    |---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
    | 7024-II | 0.766 | 0.758 | 1.17 | 1.15 | 22.3 | 22.1 |
    | Viscor     | 0.878 | 0.878 | 12.2 | 12.2 | 24.7 | 24.7 |
    | Jet A        | 0.816 | 0.858 | 1.72 | 10.0 | 27.1 | 29.5 |
    | FSJF       | 0.782 | 0.824 | 1.56 | 7.9  | 25.0 | 27.4 |

**4. Diagrams:**

NO_VISUAL_CONTENT

**5. Key Numbers, Constants Parameters:**

*   Test temperatures for FSJF and Jet A: -40°C
*   Viscosity of Viscor: 12 cS (at ambient temperatures)
*   Weber number difference between Sasol FSJF and Jet A: about 3%
*   Weber number difference for Viscor and 1074-II: about 20% higher
*Jet A: ASTM D 1655
    Sasol FSJF: DEF STAN 91-91
    Standard Calibrating Fluid: MIL-PRF-7024 Type II
    12 cS Calibrating Fluid: Viscor

title: 2010_properties_characteristics_and_performance_of_fully_synthetic_jets
text:
[VISUAL/EQUATION CONTENT - Page 12]
Here's the technical content extracted from the gas turbine research page:

1.  **Mathematical Equations:**

    *   Emission for LTO cycle = EPAP = Σ (EI,species Wfuel mode)/F00

2.  **Graphs/Charts:**

    *   **Figure 17: Spray characteristics of Sasol FSJF in an air blast atomizer**
    *   X-axis: XPAR, no units displayed
    *   Y-axis: RELATIVE SMD, SMD/SMD7024 II, no units displayed
    *   Curves: JetA, Viscor, Sasol FSJF; data points indicate relative SMD vs. XPAR

3.  **Tables:**

    *No tables in this image*

4.  **Diagrams:**

    *   **Figure 16: Comparison of fuel sprays**
        *   Components: Sasol FSJF spray, Jet A spray

5.  **Key Numbers, Constants, Parameters:**

    *   P3 = 0.3-1.7 MPa (50-250 psi (absolute))
    *   T3 = 200-480°C (460-890°F)
    *   f/a = 0.005 - 0.035
    *   Overall Pressure Ratio (OPR) = 30
    *   LTO Cycle times: 0.7 min (SLTO), 2.2 min (climb), 4.0 min (approach), 26.0 min (idle)
    *   Sasol synthetic fuel produced 4% less NOx and 19% less CO than Jet-A fuel
